title: transport-mux: cost feedback loop + session-aware proxy + codec plugin system
issueSummary: Three transport-mux integration gaps: cost records are extracted but not fully fed into SDK journal or L6 cost tracking, proxy requests lack runId/sessionId traceability, and codec discovery is hardcoded rather than pluggable. Tool schema translation in tool-mux should share normalization with transport-mux.
issueCommentsSummary: #578 is related for end-to-end cost tracking; #599 owns the transport-mux side of the feedback loop.
#591 is related for observability and distributed tracing; #599 owns session-aware proxy context.
Triage says this is staged integration/architecture work, not a quick single-file bug fix.

Triage recommends a request context contract, narrow cost/event sink, usage-bearing buffered and streaming cost emission, passthrough metrics-only behavior unless upstream usage parsing exists, explicit plugin/atlas-backed codec discovery, route-level tests, and privacy controls.
A previous closed plan PR #660 existed for the same branch name; implementation was reported in-progress and should not be assumed complete without current-state validation.
A later open plan PR #727 exists on plan/issue-599 and should be updated rather than overwritten if the branch already exists.

implementationSlices.summary: Inject a narrow transport cost/event sink, emit normalized usage once for buffered and streaming completion outcomes, and bridge to SDK journal through appendCostEventOnce semantics where run context is available.

Carry runId, sessionId, effectId, taskId, taskKind, traceparent, tracestate, request ID, and correlation ID into internal proxy context and sinks while redacting internal metadata from upstream provider requests by default.
Expose deterministic codec registration/discovery APIs backed by explicit descriptors or plugin metadata, preserving built-ins and testing duplicate, alias, and unknown codec behavior.
Share tool schema normalization between transport-mux and tool-mux without circular dependencies, covering OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google tool shapes.

acceptanceCriteria: Reuse-audit findings are rendered before design or implementation work.
Runtime call paths are recorded for buffered HTTP, streaming SSE, WebSocket, passthrough, SDK journal, agent-mux context, codec lookup, and tool schema normalization.
Tests are authored before production implementation changes and red failures are verified against issue #599 behavior.
Cost feedback emits exactly once per usage-bearing buffered completion or final streaming done event.
Cost feedback reaches SDK journal or its narrow adapter using idempotency semantics and feeds run/effect/session cost consumers where applicable.
Passthrough remains metrics-only unless upstream usage parsing is explicitly implemented and tested.
runId/sessionId/trace/correlation metadata is available to internal sinks and not forwarded to upstream providers by default.
Codec discovery is deterministic, pluggable through explicit descriptors or plugin metadata, and preserves built-in aliases.
Duplicate and unknown codec behavior is specified and tested.
Tool schema normalization is shared or bridged so tool-mux and transport-mux do not drift.
Transport-mux package build, tests, migration scorecard, SDK targeted checks, metadata verification, and diff checks pass.
nonGoals: Do not implement the entirety of #578 end-to-end cost tracking outside the transport-mux integration boundary.
Do not implement the entirety of #591 observability platform outside transport proxy context propagation.
Do not perform broad protocol/provider architecture rewrites unrelated to issue #599.
Do not add implicit arbitrary plugin code execution for codec discovery.
Do not forward internal orchestration headers upstream by default.

knownCurrentStateHints.processLibraryPath: .a5c/process-library/ was not present in the planning checkout; use library/ and nearby process files as the local process-library source.
knownCurrentStateHints.partialCostSessionImplementation: Current staging appears to contain CostFeedbackSink, CostFeedbackMetadata, x-babysitter-* extraction, cost feedback tests, appendCostEventOnce integration tests, normalizeUsage, convertTools, and codec descriptor lookup. The implementation process must validate and preserve usable partial or complete work before adding new code.
stagingEvidenceObservedDuringPlanning: packages/transport-mux/src/types.ts defines CostFeedbackMetadata, CostFeedbackRecord, and CostFeedbackSink.

packages/transport-mux/src/server.ts extracts x-babysitter-run-id, x-babysitter-session-id, x-babysitter-effect-id, x-babysitter-task-id, x-babysitter-task-kind, x-babysitter-cost-source, and x-babysitter-cost-idempotency-key into cost feedback metadata.

packages/transport-mux/tests/server.test.ts covers buffered cost feedback, streaming final-usage feedback, SDK journal appendCostEventOnce idempotency, effect cost indexing, and session budget enforcement.
packages/transport-mux/src/codecs/index.ts exposes getCodecForDescriptor, convertTools, and normalizeUsage backed by built-in codecs and aliases.
knownCurrentStateHints.currentStateRule: Each implementation slice should begin by classifying the current staging state as complete, partial, or missing. Complete slices are preserve-and-verify work and should not trigger production rewrites.
knownCurrentStateHints.priorPlanPr: PR #660 was a previous closed plan PR for issue #599; PR #727 is the current open plan PR on plan/issue-599. Both should be treated as context only, not proof of completion.
